findings.description: mask_middle with visible=0 uses value[-0:] which equals value[0:], returning a full mask prepended to the ENTIRE original plaintext (e.g. mask_middle('secret', visible=0) -> '******secret'), leaking the value the function is meant to hide.
The visible=0 negative-slice bug also makes the return length exceed len(value), violating the implicit contract that a masked value is a length-preserving redaction.

slugify does not guard negative max_length; a negative value triggers Python negative slicing (slug[:-3]) silently chopping characters from the end instead of clamping, producing a surprising non-empty result.

collapse_whitespace (and downstream slugify/truncate/etc.) has no None/type guard, so a None input raises AttributeError instead of a clear error despite the str type hint not being enforced at runtime.
levenshtein allocates the working rows sized to len(b)+1 without first swapping so b is the shorter string, so memory scales with the second argument's length rather than min(len(a),len(b)).
indent_block uses splitlines() which discards a trailing newline, so indenting text ending in '\n' loses the final blank-line/newline fidelity on round-trip.

truncate measures the limit in code points, so a suffix or text containing multi-codepoint grapheme clusters (emoji/combining marks) can be cut mid-grapheme; acceptable for char-based truncation but undocumented.
